message:
One second your dog is calmly lying on the floor… the next they're tearing around the house at full speed, ears flapping, doing wild laps like they just remembered they left the oven on. 🐶💨 We all call them "the zoomies" — but they actually have a real scientific name: Frenetic Random Activity Periods, or FRAPs.

So what's really going on? Most experts think zoomies are simply a big, joyful release of pent-up energy. After a long nap, a stretch in the crate, or an exciting moment — like you walking in the door — all that bottled-up energy comes out in one glorious burst. There's a reason the most famous zoomie of all happens right after bath time! These spells usually last only a minute or two, and they're especially common in puppies and young dogs, though plenty of older pups keep the tradition alive for life.

The good news: zoomies are a happy, healthy sign. The only thing to watch is the racetrack — slippery floors, sharp corners, stairs, and coffee-table legs are not your sprinting dog's friend. Give them a clear, safe runway and let the joy happen. 😄

So tell me: where does YOUR dog do their zoomies — the backyard, a hallway lap, or full living-room demolition derby? 🐾💛
